content.full_text:
November 24, 1987

Norrine Coyle
City Clerk and Auditor
City of Modesto
801 11th Street
Modesto, CA  95353

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-87-271

Dear Mrs. Coyle:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1/

QUESTIONS

(1) Will a candidate incur late filing penalties for failing
to include Attachment Schedule E-1 reporting payments made on
behalf of the candidate by an independent contractor? The Form
490 in question was filed in your office before the filing
deadline.

(2) If a candidate signs the verification on a Form 490 on
the line provided for the treasurer's signature, rather than on
the line provided for the candidate's signature, is the disclosure
statement considered late or not filed until the candidate's
signature appears on the correct signature line?

CONCLUSIONS

(1) Late penalties should not be assessed due to errors or
omissions on a campaign disclosure statement. Late penalties are
assessed only when a campaign statement is filed after the filing
deadline. Campaign statements required by the Act are signed
under penalty of perjury that the information provided on all of
the attached schedules is true and complete. When a candidate or
committee treasurer realizes that there is an error or omission

_________________________
1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

Norrine Coyle
Page 2


on a previously filed campaign statement, he or she is required to
amend the statement to provide all of the required information.
However, such omissions are not grounds for assessing late fines.
The candidate should amend the Form 490 to include the subvendor
information as soon as possible. There is no deadline for filing
amendments and no penalty.

     (2) The verification on the Form 490 for the candidate and
the candidate's treasurer state that both have used reasonable
diligence in preparing the statement and that to the best of their
knowledge the statement contained on the statement is true and
complete. The candidate's verification also provides that to the
best of the candidate's knowledge, his or her treasurer has used
all reasonable diligence in preparing the statement. The
candidate should amend the Form 490 to provide a signature on the
appropriate signature line but the statement should not be
considered late.

                              ANALYSIS

     Government Code Section 81004.5 provides that "any report or
statement filed pursuant to this title may be amended by the filer
at any time. Amending an incorrect or incomplete report or
statement may be considered as evidence of good faith."

     Government Code Section 81004(a) states that "all reports and
statements filed under this title shall be signed under penalty of
perjury and verified by the filer. The verification shall state
that the filer has used all reasonable diligence in its
preparation and that to the best of his knowledge it is true and
complete." Section 81004(b) further states, "A report or
statement filed by a committee which qualifies under subdivision
(a) of Section 82013 shall be signed and verified by the
treasurer." Government Code Section 84213 states, "A
candidate...shall verify his or her campaign statement and the
campaign statement of each committee subject to his or her
control...."

     In your letter, you indicate that the Form 490 in question was
signed by the candidate and the candidate's treasurer; however,
the candidate signed on the line provided for a second treasurer's
signature. Because the Form 490 filed with your office contains
both the signature of the candidate and the candidate's treasurer,
the verification requirements have been satisfied. A late filing
penalty would be appropriate only if the Form 490 were unsigned.
(In re Layton (1975) 1 FPPC, Ops. 113, copy enclosed.)

Norrine Coyle
Page 3

     Please contact me if you have additional questions concerning
this matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
By:     Mary Ann Kvasager
        Political Reform Consultant

DMG:MAK:kmt

CITY of MODESTO
INTER-OFFICE ACTION SLIP

TO ___Maryann___ OCT 23 __ U 12 [illegible] B7 DATE/10-28-87
FROM ___Norene___

☐ PLEASE HANDLE                ☐ IMMEDIATE ATTENTION        ☐ FOR YOUR INFORMATION
☐ FOR YOUR APPROVAL            ☐ PER OUR DISCUSSION         ☐ AS REQUESTED
☐ FOR YOUR COMMENTS            ☐ PLEASE RETURN              ☐ PLEASE FILE

Loved the letter more formal than I caught her at, but not forgetting a copy to my "friend." Thanks for all your help.

438/915-262/- 10/1083R
~

CITY of MODESTO
Office of City Clerk:                                    801 11th Street, P. O. Box 642, Modesto, CA 95353
(209) 577-5396                                  [TDD (209) 526-9211 Hearing and Speech Impaired only]


October 28, 1987


Ms. Mary Ann Kvasager, Consultant
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Kvasager:

     This is to confirm our telephone conversation this morning during which I
asked the following questions:

     1. When a candidate for City Council has filed a campaign statement,
Form 490, on time, but has failed to include Attachment Schedule E-1,
would the filing be considered late and the candidate required to pay the
$10 per day penalty for the number of days it takes to file the
additional schedule?

     2. If the candidate has signed the campaign statement, Form 490, in the
wrong place -- on the line for a second treasurer rather than on the
candidate's signature line -- is the form considered late or not filed
until the candidate does sign in the correct place?

     I would appreciate having the answers to these questions in writing so
that the information can easily be shared with candidates.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Norrine Coyle,
                                        City Clerk & Auditor

cc: George Petrulakis
    P. O. Box 1864
    Modesto, CA 95353
